# App Server Schema Generated by codex-cli 0.125.0: Selected Delta and Capability Notes

## Purpose

This document records the current-state delta between the vendored Codex App
Server schema for `codex-cli 0.117.0` and a locally generated schema from
`codex-cli 0.125.0`.

This is an advisory evidence map for a schema generated by `codex-cli 0.125.0`.
It does not assert a separately versioned App Server runtime, change the
compatibility policy, alter the minimum supported Codex CLI version, or select
any adoption path.

This document is not a tracking artifact by itself. Variables listed here are
current-state observations unless a separate ticket, register entry, or
implementation plan promotes them into tracked work.

Because the generated `0.125.0` schema evidence is currently scratch evidence
under `/private/tmp`, this document is provisional supporting evidence. Treat it
as a session-derived evidence map until a regenerated `0.125.0` fixture and
manifest are vendored in the repo.

## Active-Doc Disposition

The spec README links this draft as supporting evidence, so unresolved items
named here have explicit disposition:

| Category | Disposition |
|---|---|
| Tracked now | Unsupported existing `ServerRequest` reachability is tracked by [T-20260429-02](../../../tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-unsupported-server-request-reachability.md). File-change payload opacity remains tracked by [T-20260429-01](../../../tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-delegation-friction-reduction.md). |
| Explicitly not tracked by this draft | Schema-count observations, selected method/payload deltas, and capability reconnaissance are evidence only. This draft does not select adoption scope. |
| Must be tracked before a pin update | A compatibility-update ticket or implementation plan must own fixture vendoring, `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` changes, executable evidence gates, docs updates, and any `permissionProfile` migration or deferral. |
| Must be tracked before new capability adoption | Any use of MCP proxy, device-key, marketplace, guardian, or account methods needs a separate threat model and implementation plan. |

## Coverage Boundary

This is not a full schema diff. It records selected compatibility-relevant
surfaces: method counts, thread/turn request and response shapes, server-request
method and top-level payload shape changes, approval payload details used by the
plugin, selected unchanged payloads, and notification file-level churn.

The generated schema changed more broadly than the selected sections below:

| File-level measure | Count |
|---|---:|
| Vendored `0.117.0` schema files, excluding `required-methods.json` | 196 |
| Generated `0.125.0` schema files | 227 |
| Common schema files | 195 |
| Changed common schema files | 63 |
| Added schema files | 32 |
| Removed schema files | 1 |

Removed schema file:

- `v2/ThreadRealtimeTranscriptUpdatedNotification.json`

These counts are a churn signal, not a semantic completeness claim. They are
also recorded in the generated comparison report named below.

| Current vendored fixture | `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0/` |

| Observed dirty state | `docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/README.md` modified; schema-delta doc, comparison evidence, reachability ticket, and comparison script untracked |
| Live compatibility check | `uv run pytest packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat_live.py -v` -> `14 passed` |
| Vendored contract check | `uv run pytest packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat.py -v` -> `43 passed` |

The generated `0.125.0` schemas under `/private/tmp` are scratch evidence from
this local session, not repo artifacts. The recheck tree and clean-room
reproduction tree were generated during this document revision and matched the
user-generated tree by canonical digest.

Scratch schema generation does not create `required-methods.json`. That manifest
is generated by `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/scripts/regenerate_schema.sh`
when a schema is vendored under `tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/<version>/`.

## Validation Matrix

| Surface | Evidence in this document | Boundary |
|---|---|---|
| Local binary identity | `codex --version` reported `codex-cli 0.125.0` | Validates the local binary used for scratch generation only |
| Vendored fixture contract | `test_codex_compat.py` reported `43 passed` | Validates the current `0.117.0` fixture and manifest behavior |
| Live method probe | `test_codex_compat_live.py` reported `14 passed` | Validates required and optional method presence against the installed binary |
| `ClientRequest` method surface | Extracted from `ClientRequest.json`: 59 old, 71 new, 12 added, none removed | Method presence only; no payload or behavior guarantee |
| `ServerRequest` method surface | Extracted from `ServerRequest.json`: 9 old, 9 new, none added, none removed | Method surface only; selected params are checked separately |
| Selected schema equality | Response and params schemas listed under "Unchanged Shapes Checked" compare equal as parsed JSON | Equality for the listed files only |
| Thread and turn request payload schemas | Focused property comparison for `thread/start`, `turn/start`, `thread/resume`, and `thread/fork` params | Schema shape only; no runtime behavior proof |
| Current `sandboxPolicy` runtime behavior | Related T-01 smoke and diagnostic records exist outside this document | Not reproduced here; not proof of `permissionProfile` equivalence |
| `sandboxPolicy` versus `permissionProfile` semantics | `0.125.0` schema descriptions state mutual exclusion on relevant request paths | Description-level evidence only; runtime rejection, precedence, and behavioral equivalence are unvalidated |

## Current Repo Baseline

The compatibility source of truth is
`packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/codex_compat.py`.

Current required App Server methods:

| Tier | Methods |
|---|---|
| Required | `thread/start`, `thread/resume`, `thread/fork`, `thread/read`, `turn/start`, `turn/interrupt` |
| Optional | `turn/steer` |

Current runtime request construction lives in
`packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/runtime.py`:

| Runtime path | Current shape |
|---|---|
| `initialize` | Sends `clientInfo` only |
| `thread/start` | Sends `cwd`, `approvalPolicy`, `personality`, `serviceName` |
| Advisory `turn/start` | Sends `sandboxPolicy={"type": "readOnly"}`, `approvalPolicy="never"` |
| Execution `turn/start` | Sends `sandboxPolicy=<workspaceWrite policy>`, default `approvalPolicy="on-request"` |
| `thread/read` | Sends `threadId` and `includeTurns=True` |
| `turn/interrupt` | Sends `threadId`; includes `turnId` only when available |

Current execution sandbox construction uses the older `sandboxPolicy` model,
including `workspaceWrite`, writable roots, restricted read-only roots, platform
defaults, disabled network access, and tmp exclusions.

## Selected Schema Delta: 0.117.0 to 0.125.0

### ClientRequest Method Surface

| Measure | `0.117.0` | `0.125.0` |
|---|---:|---:|
| `ClientRequest` methods | 59 | 71 |
| Removed methods | 0 | 0 |
| Added methods | - | 12 |

Added methods:

| Method | Capability area |
|---|---|
| `account/sendAddCreditsNudgeEmail` | Account or billing-related notification path |
| `device/key/create` | Device key creation |
| `device/key/public` | Device public key read/export |
| `device/key/sign` | Device key signing |
| `marketplace/add` | Marketplace add/install path |
| `marketplace/remove` | Marketplace remove path |
| `marketplace/upgrade` | Marketplace upgrade path |
| `mcpServer/resource/read` | MCP server resource read proxy |
| `mcpServer/tool/call` | MCP server tool call proxy |
| `thread/approveGuardianDeniedAction` | Guardian-denied action approval path |
| `thread/inject_items` | Thread item injection |
| `thread/turns/list` | Thread turn listing |

The existing required and optional methods are still present in the generated
`0.125.0` schema.

### ServerRequest Method Surface

| Measure | `0.117.0` | `0.125.0` |
|---|---:|---:|
| `ServerRequest` methods | 9 | 9 |
| Removed methods | 0 | 0 |
| Added methods | 0 | 0 |

Unchanged server-initiated methods:

- `account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh`
- `applyPatchApproval`
- `execCommandApproval`
- `item/commandExecution/requestApproval`
- `item/fileChange/requestApproval`
- `item/permissions/requestApproval`
- `item/tool/call`
- `item/tool/requestUserInput`
- `mcpServer/elicitation/request`

The plugin currently maps the server-initiated approval methods it consumes in
`approval_router.py`.

Top-level params and response checks for all server-initiated methods:

| Method | Params result | Response result |
|---|---|---|
| `item/commandExecution/requestApproval` | Top-level properties and required fields unchanged; nested schema changed | Equal |
| `item/fileChange/requestApproval` | Equal | Equal |
| `item/tool/requestUserInput` | Equal | Equal |
| `mcpServer/elicitation/request` | Equal | Equal |
| `item/permissions/requestApproval` | Added required top-level `cwd` | Added optional `strictAutoReview` |
| `item/tool/call` | Added top-level `namespace`; required fields unchanged | Equal |
| `account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh` | Equal | Equal |
| `applyPatchApproval` | Equal | Top-level properties and required fields unchanged; nested schema changed |
| `execCommandApproval` | Equal | Top-level properties and required fields unchanged; nested schema changed |

This table covers top-level params/response shape and parsed equality. It does
not claim that every nested definition in every server-request payload has been
semantically reviewed.

Server-request delta attribution and reachability:

| Method | Delta attribution | Reachability classification | Current handler support |
|---|---|---|---|
| `item/commandExecution/requestApproval` | Existing method; `0.125.0` nested params changed | Current delegated-execution flow when command approval is requested | Supported and parked |
| `item/fileChange/requestApproval` | Existing method; checked params/response unchanged | Current delegated-execution flow when file-change approval is requested | Supported and parked |
| `item/tool/requestUserInput` | Existing method; checked params/response unchanged | Current delegated-execution flow when Codex asks for operator input | Supported and parked |
| `mcpServer/elicitation/request` | Existing method; checked params/response unchanged | Theoretically possible if Codex-side MCP elicitation is active; not a current plugin flow | Unsupported parser shape |
| `item/permissions/requestApproval` | Existing method with `0.125.0` params/response delta | Theoretically possible during delegated execution if App Server asks for dynamic permission approval | Parsed as unsupported `unknown` |
| `item/tool/call` | Existing method with `0.125.0` params delta | Theoretically possible in tool-mediated Codex flows; not a current plugin flow | Unsupported parser shape |
| `account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh` | Existing method; checked params/response unchanged | Auth/runtime support surface, not a delegated-execution feature | Unsupported parser shape |
| `applyPatchApproval` | Existing method; `0.125.0` response nested schema changed | Alternate approval surface, not the current plugin approval protocol | Unsupported parser shape |
| `execCommandApproval` | Existing method; `0.125.0` response nested schema changed | Alternate approval surface, not the current plugin approval protocol | Unsupported parser shape |

Unsupported handling for these methods is therefore split across two concerns:
latent current-runtime debt for existing emitted requests, and true `0.125.0`
payload changes for methods whose schemas changed. The schema delta does not by
itself prove whether any unsupported method is reachable in normal advisory or
delegated execution.

Current runtime compatibility for emitted server requests:

| Method | Schema changed? | Current parser accepts? | Current runtime behavior if emitted during delegated execution | Required test before pin upgrade |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `item/commandExecution/requestApproval` | Params nested schema changed; response equal | Yes; mapped to `command_approval` | Parked for operator decision | Regression test for capture, projection, decision dispatch, and nested permission preservation |
| `item/fileChange/requestApproval` | Params and response equal | Yes; mapped to `file_change` | Parked for operator decision | Regression test for capture, projection, decision dispatch, and non-empty payload visibility when available |
| `item/tool/requestUserInput` | Params and response equal | Yes; mapped to `request_user_input` | Parked for operator decision | Regression test for capture and approve/deny response payloads |
| `mcpServer/elicitation/request` | Params and response equal | No; lacks `itemId` and has nullable/non-required `turnId` | Parse-failure path creates minimal `unknown`, interrupts the turn, and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Synthetic or live emitted-request test proving supported handling or intentional safe terminal behavior |
| `item/permissions/requestApproval` | Params add required `cwd`; response adds optional `strictAutoReview` | Shape accepted, but method maps to `unknown` | Known-parsed non-parkable path interrupts the turn and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Test for support or intentional safe terminal behavior, including `strictAutoReview` response semantics |
| `item/tool/call` | Params add optional `namespace`; response equal | No; lacks `itemId` | Parse-failure path creates minimal `unknown`, interrupts the turn, and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Synthetic or live emitted-request test proving supported handling or intentional safe terminal behavior |
| `account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh` | Params and response equal | No; lacks `itemId`, `threadId`, and `turnId` | Parse-failure path creates minimal `unknown`, interrupts the turn, and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Synthetic or live emitted-request test proving supported handling or intentional safe terminal behavior |
| `applyPatchApproval` | Params equal; response nested schema changed | No; lacks `itemId`, `threadId`, and `turnId` | Parse-failure path creates minimal `unknown`, interrupts the turn, and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Synthetic or live emitted-request test proving supported handling or intentional safe terminal behavior |
| `execCommandApproval` | Params equal; response nested schema changed | No; lacks `itemId`, `threadId`, and `turnId` | Parse-failure path creates minimal `unknown`, interrupts the turn, and can terminalize the job as `unknown` | Synthetic or live emitted-request test proving supported handling or intentional safe terminal behavior |

During `_run_turn()`, `runtime.py` ignores method-less messages, stores
method-bearing messages as notifications, requires `params` to be an object,
skips messages whose `turnId` is present and different from the current turn,
and then invokes the server-request handler only when the message has an `id`
and a handler was provided. The current parser in `approval_router.py`
hard-requires `itemId`, `threadId`, and `turnId`, then maps only three methods
to parkable kinds. Therefore, schema equality in the first table is not
equivalent to runtime compatibility if an unsupported server request is emitted
and passes the runtime filters.

### Thread and Turn Request Shapes

The `0.125.0` schema adds permission/profile fields to thread and turn creation
paths.

| Schema file | Added properties | Removed properties | Required field change |
|---|---|---|---|
| `v2/ThreadStartParams.json` | `permissionProfile`, `sessionStartSource` | none | none |
| `v2/TurnStartParams.json` | `permissionProfile` | none | none |
| `v2/ThreadResumeParams.json` | `excludeTurns`, `permissionProfile` | none | none |
| `v2/ThreadForkParams.json` | `excludeTurns`, `permissionProfile` | none | none |

`ApprovalsReviewer` also adds `auto_review` to the older enum values `user` and
`guardian_subagent`.

### PermissionProfile Shape

`permissionProfile` is new in the generated `0.125.0` thread and turn schemas.
The generated schema descriptions describe it as a full permissions override:

| Request path | Description-level relationship to older field |
|---|---|
| `thread/start` | `permissionProfile` description says it cannot be combined with `sandbox` |
| `turn/start` | `permissionProfile` description says it cannot be combined with `sandboxPolicy` |

The inspected JSON Schema files do not encode this as machine-enforced
`oneOf`, `not`, dependency, or equivalent validation logic at the params-schema
level. Treat rejection, precedence, and runtime equivalence as unvalidated until
runtime behavior is proven.

The generated `PermissionProfile` definition has three alternatives:

| Profile type | Required fields | Meaning in schema terms |
|---|---|---|
| `managed` | `type`, `fileSystem`, `network` | Codex owns sandbox construction for the profile |
| `disabled` | `type` | No outer sandbox |
| `external` | `type`, `network` | Filesystem isolation is enforced by an external caller |

The managed filesystem shape can express:

| Element | Shape |
|---|---|
| Filesystem permissions | `restricted` with `entries`, or `unrestricted` |
| Entry access modes | `read`, `write`, `none` |
| Entry path types | absolute path, glob pattern, special path |
| Optional depth control | `globScanMaxDepth` |

The current plugin does not construct or send `permissionProfile`.

### Thread Response Permission View

`ThreadStartResponse.json`, `ThreadResumeResponse.json`, and
`ThreadForkResponse.json` changed in the generated `0.125.0` schema:

| Schema files | Added properties | Required field change | Permission evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `v2/ThreadStartResponse.json`, `v2/ThreadResumeResponse.json`, `v2/ThreadForkResponse.json` | `instructionSources`, `permissionProfile` | none | `permissionProfile` is described as the canonical active permissions view; `sandbox` is retained as a legacy compatibility field |

Current `runtime.py` extracts only `thread.id` from start, fork, and resume
responses. It does not read `permissionProfile`, `sandbox`, or
`instructionSources` from these response payloads.

### Direct Runtime-Consumed Changed Schemas

The following changed common schemas are directly consumed, inspected, or
preserved by current runtime paths. In each case, the top-level properties and
required fields are stable between `0.117.0` and `0.125.0`, but nested schema
definitions changed.

| Schema file | Current consumer path | Top-level result | Runtime boundary |
|---|---|---|---|
| `v2/TurnStartResponse.json` | `runtime.py` reads `turn.id` after `turn/start` | Properties and required fields unchanged | Nested `turn` schema changed; current code only validates `turn.id` |
| `v2/ThreadReadResponse.json` | `runtime.py` returns `thread/read`; dialogue paths consume returned thread/turn data | Properties and required fields unchanged | Nested thread/turn shapes changed; this document does not validate dialogue extraction against new nested shapes |
| `v2/ItemCompletedNotification.json` | `_run_turn()` reads completed `agentMessage` text from `item/completed` | Properties and required fields unchanged | Nested `item` schema changed; current code only reads `item.type` and `item.text` |
| `v2/TurnCompletedNotification.json` | `_run_turn()` reads terminal `turn.status` from `turn/completed` | Properties and required fields unchanged | Nested `turn` schema changed; current code only validates terminal status |
| `v2/TurnStartedNotification.json` | Notifications are preserved in `TurnExecutionResult.notifications` | Properties and required fields unchanged | No direct branch in `runtime.py`; consumers may inspect preserved notification payloads |
| `v2/ItemStartedNotification.json` | Notifications are preserved in `TurnExecutionResult.notifications` | Properties and required fields unchanged | No direct branch in `runtime.py`; consumers may inspect preserved notification payloads |
| `v2/ThreadStartedNotification.json` | May be buffered by the JSON-RPC client around thread lifecycle requests | Properties and required fields unchanged | No direct branch in `runtime.py`; this document does not validate buffered-notification consumers |

### Notification File Churn

Notification method behavior is not fully analyzed here. File-level notification
churn in the parsed comparison:

| Measure | Count |
|---|---:|
| Added notification schema files | 8 |
| Removed notification schema files | 1 |
| Changed common notification schema files | 15 |

Added notification schema files:

- `v2/ExternalAgentConfigImportCompletedNotification.json`
- `v2/FileChangePatchUpdatedNotification.json`
- `v2/GuardianWarningNotification.json`
- `v2/ModelVerificationNotification.json`
- `v2/ThreadRealtimeSdpNotification.json`
- `v2/ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDeltaNotification.json`
- `v2/ThreadRealtimeTranscriptDoneNotification.json`
- `v2/WarningNotification.json`

Removed notification schema file:

- `v2/ThreadRealtimeTranscriptUpdatedNotification.json`

Changed common notification schemas consumed or preserved today:

- `v2/ItemCompletedNotification.json`
- `v2/ItemStartedNotification.json`
- `v2/ThreadStartedNotification.json`
- `v2/TurnCompletedNotification.json`
- `v2/TurnStartedNotification.json`

The current runtime stores notifications generically and has specific behavior
for `item/completed`, `turn/completed`, and server-initiated requests. Both
`item/completed` and `turn/completed` are changed common schemas in this
comparison, even though their top-level properties and required fields are
stable. This document does not validate operator-visibility semantics for the
added warning, guardian, model-verification, file-change patch, or realtime
notification surfaces.

### Command Approval Payload Shape

`CommandExecutionRequestApprovalParams.json` has no top-level property changes
and no required-field changes.

Unchanged top-level required fields:

- `itemId`
- `threadId`
- `turnId`

Nested shape changes in the generated `0.125.0` schema:

| Area | `0.117.0` | `0.125.0` | Boundary |
|---|---|---|
| `cwd` | `string | null` | `AbsolutePathBuf | null` | Command approval params |
| `ReadCommandAction.path` | `string` | `AbsolutePathBuf` | Required in both versions |
| `ListFilesCommandAction.path` | `string | null` | `string | null` | Unchanged |
| `SearchCommandAction.path` | `string | null` | `string | null` | Unchanged |
| `UnknownCommandAction.path` | absent | absent | Unchanged |
| `AdditionalFileSystemPermissions` | `read`, `write` | `entries`, `globScanMaxDepth`, `read`, `write` | `read` and `write` remain but are described as future-removal fields in `0.125.0` |
| `AdditionalPermissionProfile.macos` | present | absent | Removed from inspected command-approval params and `ServerRequest` definitions |
| macOS-specific permission definitions | `AdditionalMacOsPermissions`, `MacOsAutomationPermission`, `MacOsContactsPermission`, `MacOsPreferencesPermission` present | absent | No `MacOs` schema definitions were found in the generated `0.125.0` command-approval or `ServerRequest` schema files |

The current approval parser extracts the context fields (`id`, `method`,
`itemId`, `threadId`, `turnId`) and preserves other request fields in
`requested_scope`. That means these nested schema changes affect projection and
display fidelity more directly than basic request parsing.

The command-action path delta is variant-specific. It does not imply that every
`commandActions[].path` value is absolute or non-null.

## Unchanged Shapes Checked

The following schemas were semantically equal between the vendored `0.117.0`
fixture and the generated `0.125.0` schema:

| Schema | Current relevance |
|---|---|
| `ToolRequestUserInputResponse.json` | `request_user_input` approve/deny response payloads |
| `CommandExecutionRequestApprovalResponse.json` | command approval accept/decline/cancel response payloads |
| `FileChangeRequestApprovalResponse.json` | file-change approval accept/decline/cancel response payloads |
| `ToolRequestUserInputParams.json` | parked user-input request capture |
| `FileChangeRequestApprovalParams.json` | parked file-change request capture |
| `v1/InitializeParams.json` | App Server initialize request |
| `v1/InitializeResponse.json` | App Server initialize response |
| `v2/ThreadReadParams.json` | thread state and turn history read |
| `v2/TurnInterruptParams.json` | turn interruption |
| `v2/TurnSteerParams.json` | optional steering capability |

This comparison does not prove all runtime semantics are unchanged. It only
records equality for the checked JSON Schema files.

## Implicated Files

### Production Code

| File | Why it is implicated |
|---|---|
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/codex_compat.py` | Owns `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION`, `MINIMUM_CODEX_VERSION`, required/optional method sets, live method probing, and exact-version capability behavior |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/scripts/regenerate_schema.sh` | Generates versioned vendored schema fixtures and `required-methods.json` |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/runtime.py` | Constructs `initialize`, `thread/start`, `thread/fork`, `turn/start`, `thread/read`, `thread/resume`, and `turn/interrupt` payloads |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/control_plane.py` | Reports compatibility status, available methods, runtime bootstrap errors, and App Server user-agent version |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/approval_router.py` | Maps server-request methods to pending request kinds and preserves request payload details in `requested_scope` |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/delegation_controller.py` | Builds App Server approval response payloads and documents the pinned `request_user_input` response shape |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/models.py` | Carries runtime handshake, method availability, advisory runtime state, and pending request records |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/server/jsonrpc_client.py` | Generic transport layer for App Server request, notification, and response flow |

### Fixtures and Tests

| File or directory | Why it is implicated |
|---|---|
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0/` | Current vendored schema fixture |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.125.0/` | Expected location for a regenerated `0.125.0` fixture if the tested pin changes |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/conftest.py` | Resolves the fixture directory from `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat.py` | Contract tests against vendored fixture and manifest |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat_live.py` | Live binary version and method-surface checks |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_control_plane.py` | Uses fake compatibility results and fake App Server user-agent strings |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_control_plane_live.py` | Live status check against installed Codex binary |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_delegate_start_integration.py` | Uses fake compatibility results for execution runtime bootstrap tests |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_delegate_decide_async_integration.py` | Pins expected approval response payload shapes and comments referencing the fixture path |

### Docs and Tracking Artifacts

| File | Why it is implicated |
|---|---|
| `docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/delivery.md` | Contains the current compatibility policy, tested baseline, minimum accepted version, and upgrade workflow |
| `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/README.md` | States the Codex CLI prerequisite |
| `docs/tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-delegation-friction-reduction.md` | References the current `0.117.0` schema fixture for file-change shape investigation |
| `docs/diagnostics/2026-04-28-delegate-execution-diagnostic.md` | Historical diagnostic record that explicitly notes the `0.117.0` fixture during a `0.125.0` live run |
| `docs/plans/2026-04-24-packet-1-deferred-approval-response/` | Historical Packet 1 records that cite the pinned `0.117.0` response shape |

Historical diagnostic and plan artifacts may intentionally preserve the version
that was true when they were written. Current-facing docs and active tickets
are the places where version references can become operationally stale.

## Tracking Disposition

This draft delegates tracked work to separate artifacts. It records the
following tracking posture for the unresolved surfaces it names:

| Surface | Current disposition | Tracking artifact if the version pin changes |
|---|---|---|
| Fixture and tested-version refresh | Untracked here; policy remains in `delivery.md` and `codex_compat.py` | A dedicated compatibility-update ticket or implementation plan that vendors `tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.125.0/`, updates `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION`, and records verification |
| Minimum supported Codex CLI version | Untracked here; no minimum-floor change selected | Same compatibility-update artifact, if the minimum floor is considered |
| `permissionProfile` versus `sandboxPolicy` | Untracked here; description-level schema evidence only | Compatibility-update artifact must include a runtime behavior test or explicitly defer `permissionProfile` adoption |
| Existing unsupported server-request reachability | Tracked by [T-20260429-02](../../../tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-unsupported-server-request-reachability.md); latent current-runtime risk, not automatically a `0.125.0` delta | Compatibility-update artifact must link the ticket disposition and classify emitted unsupported requests as current flow, theoretical, or unrelated |
| `0.125.0` server-request payload changes | Untracked here; selected changed params/responses are classified as pre-pin-upgrade gates | Compatibility-update artifact must include tests or explicit non-goal disposition for changed server-request payloads |
| Warning, guardian, model-verification, file-change patch, and realtime notification visibility | Untracked here; classified as pre-pin-upgrade gates | Compatibility-update artifact must include operator-visible notification checks or an explicit non-goal disposition |
| File-change payload opacity | Already tracked by `docs/tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-delegation-friction-reduction.md` | Phase 2 of that ticket needs to inspect the currently tested fixture and any generated newer schema relevant to the version under investigation |
| Current-facing docs that name `0.117.0` | Untracked here unless a pin change begins | Compatibility-update artifact must update `delivery.md`, `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/README.md`, active ticket references, and fixture-path comments as part of the same change |

If a compatibility-update artifact is created, update
`docs/status/codex-collaboration-reconciliation-register.md` after source
artifacts have been patched and verified. The register summarizes the
source-truth state; it is not the first home for the detailed upgrade evidence.

## Pre-Pin-Upgrade Evidence Gates

This draft is not sufficient evidence to update `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` by
itself. Before using it for a pin update, the compatibility-update artifact
needs executable evidence for the gates below:

| Gate | Blocking before pin update? | Executable evidence target |
|---|---|---|
| Vendored fixture regeneration | Yes | `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/scripts/regenerate_schema.sh 0.125.0`; generated fixture under `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.125.0/` including `required-methods.json` |
| Schema comparison report | Yes | `python3 packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/scripts/compare_app_server_schemas.py --old-root packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0 --new-root packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.125.0 --output docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/evidence/<pin-update-schema-comparison>.json` |
| Contract tests | Yes | `uv run pytest packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat.py -v` against the vendored `0.125.0` fixture |
| Live method probe | Yes | `uv run pytest packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/test_codex_compat_live.py -v` against the installed binary |
| Advisory smoke | Yes | Live or automated advisory smoke record showing thread start and read-only turn completion; if automated, add a focused live test under `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/` and run it with `uv run pytest <test-file> -v` |
| Execution smoke | Yes | Live delegation smoke record showing worktree artifact creation and completion or escalation through the current approval protocol; record the run artifact path in the compatibility-update artifact |
| `thread/read` dialogue and recovery extraction | Yes | Fixture-backed or live regression under `packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/` covering completed-turn status filtering, next-turn sequence derivation, `DialogueController.read()` agent-message extraction, timestamp extraction, metadata integrity handling, and best-effort recovery confirmation |
| `sandboxPolicy` / `permissionProfile` behavior | Yes | Runtime proof that current `sandboxPolicy` behavior remains accepted, or focused tests for any `permissionProfile` migration covering rejection, precedence, and response active-permission reporting |
| Existing unsupported server-request reachability | Yes | T-20260429-02 disposition plus fixture-backed or live tests proving reachable methods are supported or intentionally terminal, and unreachable methods have method-by-method evidence |
| `0.125.0` server-request payload changes | Yes | Focused tests for changed server-request params/responses, including command nested permissions, `item/permissions/requestApproval` `cwd` and `strictAutoReview`, `item/tool/call.namespace`, and nested apply/exec approval response changes |
| Operator-visible notifications | Yes | Focused tests or live records for warning, guardian, model-verification, file-change patch, and changed consumed notifications, with projection behavior or explicit non-goal disposition |
| Current-facing docs | Yes | `rg -n "0\\.117\\.0|0\\.125\\.0|TESTED_CODEX_VERSION|codex-cli" docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration packages/plugins/codex-collaboration docs/tickets` reviewed with stale operational references patched |
| New capability adoption threat model | Conditional | Required before implementing MCP proxy, device-key, marketplace, guardian, or account methods; not required for a pin update that does not adopt those methods |

## Capability Surface Map

This section is non-tracking reconnaissance. It lists capability areas exposed
by the selected `0.125.0` delta so future work can find the relevant files and
verification surfaces. It is not an implementation plan, backlog, priority
order, or scope selection. Do not treat any area below as planned work unless a
separate ticket, register entry, or implementation plan promotes it.

Security boundary: the MCP proxy, device-key, marketplace, guardian, and account
surfaces are reconnaissance only in this document. They must not be implemented
or exposed by codex-collaboration without a separate threat model, credential
boundary review, audit/projection plan, and explicit user-facing use case.

### Area A: Vendored Contract Refresh

Current input:

- Local binary is `codex-cli 0.125.0`.
- Generated schema exists at `/private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0/`.
- Required and optional methods are present in the generated schema.

Files in scope:

- `server/codex_compat.py`
- `scripts/regenerate_schema.sh`
- `tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/<version>/`
- `tests/conftest.py`
- `tests/test_codex_compat.py`
- `tests/test_codex_compat_live.py`
- current-facing docs that name the tested baseline

Verification inventory:

- Contract fixture tests
- Live compatibility tests
- Manifest version/readback checks

### Area B: Compatibility Semantics

Current input:

- `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` and `MINIMUM_CODEX_VERSION` are separate constants.
- Versions below `MINIMUM_CODEX_VERSION` fail closed.
- Exact matches to `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` can populate capability claims from
  the vendored method set.
- Non-exact versions rely on live compatibility probing when the control plane
  uses `check_live_runtime_compatibility`.

Files in scope:

- `server/codex_compat.py`
- `server/control_plane.py`
- `tests/test_codex_compat.py`
- `tests/test_codex_compat_live.py`
- `docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/delivery.md`

Open policy variables:

- Minimum accepted Codex CLI version
- Exact-tested-version behavior
- Status reporting for non-exact versions
- Which new methods, if any, become required or optional plugin capabilities

### Area C: PermissionProfile Assessment

Current input:

- `0.125.0` adds `permissionProfile` to `thread/start`, `turn/start`,
  `thread/resume`, and `thread/fork`.
- `permissionProfile` descriptions say it cannot be combined with `sandbox` or
  `sandboxPolicy` on the paths where those older fields exist.
- Current plugin runtime paths still use `sandboxPolicy`.
- Existing T-01 smoke and diagnostic records are related evidence for current
  `sandboxPolicy` behavior under the local `0.125.0` binary, but they are
  separate from this schema comparison and do not validate `permissionProfile`.

Files in scope:

- `server/runtime.py`
- `tests/test_control_plane.py`
- execution/delegation tests that assert sandbox behavior
- diagnostic records for workspace-write and platform-default behavior

Verification inventory:

- Advisory read-only runtime smoke
- Execution workspace-write artifact smoke
- Network denial probe
- sensitive-host-path read denial probe
- sibling-worktree read denial probe
- App Server request payload capture or schema validation where available

### Area D: Approval Projection and Operator Visibility

Current input:

- `CommandExecutionRequestApprovalParams` keeps the same top-level required
  fields but changes nested permission details.
- `AdditionalFileSystemPermissions` can now include `entries` and
  `globScanMaxDepth`.
- Active friction tracking records file-change payload opacity as an operator
  visibility issue.

Files in scope:

- `server/approval_router.py`
- `server/delegation_controller.py`
- pending request store/model tests
- poll projection tests
- `docs/tickets/2026-04-29-codex-collaboration-delegation-friction-reduction.md`

Verification inventory:

- Captured command approval request with permission details
- Captured file-change approval request
- Poll projection readback
- Decide response round trip
- Timeout response or interrupt path checks

### Area E: Thread State and Context Capabilities

Current input:

- `thread/turns/list` is newly present.
- `thread/inject_items` is newly present.
- Current code uses `thread/read` with `includeTurns=True`.
- Current advisory coherence uses stale-context marking and next-turn context
  injection rather than direct thread item injection.

Files in scope:

- `server/runtime.py`
- `server/control_plane.py`
- dialogue controller code
- context assembly code
- recovery and journal tests

Verification inventory:

- Thread history equivalence checks
- turn listing pagination or filtering checks, if the schema exposes them
- injected item visibility checks
- stale-context and lineage interactions

### Area F: MCP Server Proxy Capabilities

Current input:

- `mcpServer/resource/read` is newly present.
- `mcpServer/tool/call` is newly present.
- The plugin already exposes its own Claude-facing MCP server.
- The App Server methods appear to expose Codex-side MCP server interaction.

Files in scope:

- `server/runtime.py`
- `server/control_plane.py`
- `server/mcp_server.py`
- safety and credential scanning paths for content-bearing tool calls

Verification inventory:

- Tool call request and response shape capture
- resource read request and response shape capture
- safety-policy boundary checks
- audit event shape checks
- threat model before any implementation or exposure

### Area G: Guardian, Marketplace, Device Key, and Account Methods

Current input:

- `thread/approveGuardianDeniedAction` is newly present.
- `device/key/create`, `device/key/public`, and `device/key/sign` are newly
  present.
- `marketplace/add`, `marketplace/remove`, and `marketplace/upgrade` are newly
  present.
- `account/sendAddCreditsNudgeEmail` is newly present.

Files in scope:

- No direct production call sites currently exist in `codex-collaboration`.
- Future scope would depend on a concrete plugin-facing use case.

Verification inventory:

- Schema inspection
- live method availability check
- capability gating behavior
- auth/account boundary checks, where relevant
- threat model before any implementation or exposure

## Reproduction Appendix

For clean-room scratch reproduction, generate into a fresh empty directory and
use the printed path as the new-schema root in the comparison commands below:

```bash
SCHEMA_TMP="$(mktemp -d /private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-repro.XXXXXX)"
test -z "$(find "$SCHEMA_TMP" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print -quit)"
codex app-server generate-json-schema --out "$SCHEMA_TMP"
printf '%s\n' "$SCHEMA_TMP"
```

The scratch recheck path retained for this revision was generated with:

```bash
codex app-server generate-json-schema --out /private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-codex-doc-recheck
```

A later clean-room reproduction used the `mktemp -d` command above and produced
`/private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-cleanroom.2onDzw/`; the directory
was empty before generation.

Canonical tree checksums were computed from each JSON file's relative path plus
parsed JSON serialized with sorted keys and compact separators.

Checksum command:

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import hashlib
import json
from pathlib import Path

cases = [
    (
        "old_all",
        Path("packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0"),
        False,
    ),
    (
        "old_schema_only",
        Path("packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0"),
        True,
    ),
    (
        "user_tmp",
        Path("/private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0"),
        True,
    ),
    (
        "recheck_tmp",
        Path("/private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-codex-doc-recheck"),
        True,
    ),
    (
        "cleanroom_tmp",
        Path("/private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-cleanroom.2onDzw"),
        True,
    ),
]

for label, root, exclude_manifest in cases:
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    count = 0
    for path in sorted(root.rglob("*.json")):
        if exclude_manifest and path.name == "required-methods.json":
            continue
        rel = path.relative_to(root).as_posix()
        data = json.loads(path.read_text())
        canonical = json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))
        digest.update(rel.encode())
        digest.update(b"\0")
        digest.update(canonical.encode())
        digest.update(b"\0")
        count += 1
    print(f"{label}: files={count} canonical_tree_sha256={digest.hexdigest()}")
PY
```

| Tree | JSON files | `required-methods.json` | Canonical tree SHA-256 |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Vendored `0.117.0`, all JSON | 197 | present | `f533e52c6a5839a3d27ad96e9e8fd898011402997e0704fd5cd3e9d755a77ee1` |
| Vendored `0.117.0`, schema files only | 196 | excluded | `51d10bb7c327619205171c76ba0164a2f9bd62bd0fee37c3e6f2498b3c0b46b9` |
| User-generated `0.125.0` scratch tree | 227 | absent | `41e0dd76f24eafe79edbc188dfdd75416aa577c469ea91b061c0597de043109d` |
| Revision recheck `0.125.0` scratch tree | 227 | absent | `41e0dd76f24eafe79edbc188dfdd75416aa577c469ea91b061c0597de043109d` |
| Clean-room reproduction `0.125.0` scratch tree | 227 | absent | `41e0dd76f24eafe79edbc188dfdd75416aa577c469ea91b061c0597de043109d` |

The detailed comparison logic is now in the repo script:

```bash
python3 packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/scripts/compare_app_server_schemas.py \
  --old-root packages/plugins/codex-collaboration/tests/fixtures/codex-app-server/0.117.0 \
  --new-root /private/tmp/codex-app-server-schema-0.125.0-cleanroom.2onDzw \
  --output docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/evidence/2026-04-29-codex-app-server-schema-0.117.0-to-0.125.0-comparison.json
```

The generated report contains:

- canonical tree digests
- file-level churn lists and counts
- `ClientRequest` and `ServerRequest` method surface deltas
- `ApprovalsReviewer` enum changes
- thread/turn request and response shape summaries
- direct runtime-consumed changed schema summaries
- server-request params and response shape summaries
- command approval nested permission and command-action details
- selected parsed-equality checks

Observed report summary:

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Report file | `docs/superpowers/specs/codex-collaboration/evidence/2026-04-29-codex-app-server-schema-0.117.0-to-0.125.0-comparison.json` |
| File-level schema churn | old schema files 196, new schema files 227, common 195, changed common 63, added 32, removed 1 |
| Removed schema file | `v2/ThreadRealtimeTranscriptUpdatedNotification.json` |
| Notification file churn | added 8, removed 1, changed common 15 |
| `ClientRequest` | `old=59`, `new=71`, added 12, removed none |
| `ServerRequest` | `old=9`, `new=9`, added none, removed none |
| `ApprovalsReviewer` | added `auto_review` |
| Selected unchanged schemas | all files listed under "Unchanged Shapes Checked" compared equal as parsed JSON |

## Unresolved Variables

These variables are intentionally unresolved in this document. Except where
the Active-Doc Disposition or Tracking Disposition sections link a separate
ticket, they are non-tracking observations here.

| Variable | Relevant evidence |
|---|---|
| Whether `TESTED_CODEX_VERSION` changes to `0.125.0` | Local binary is `0.125.0`; live compatibility passes; generated schema differs from vendored fixture |
| Whether `MINIMUM_CODEX_VERSION` remains `0.117.0` or changes | The constants are separate; altering the minimum changes runtime acceptance behavior |
| Whether any newly added method becomes a required or optional plugin capability | Added methods are present; no current plugin call sites consume them |
| Whether runtime payloads migrate from `sandboxPolicy` to `permissionProfile` | `permissionProfile` exists in `0.125.0`; current runtime uses `sandboxPolicy`; schema descriptions say they cannot be combined |
| Whether `thread/turns/list` replaces or supplements `thread/read` | Both methods are present in `0.125.0`; current code uses `thread/read` |
| Whether `thread/inject_items` affects stale-context handling | New method exists; current design uses Claude-side context assembly and stale markers |
| Whether richer command permission data changes operator-facing projection | Nested command approval permission schema changed; parser currently preserves payload fields opaquely |
| Current-facing docs affected if the tested schema changes | `delivery.md`, README, active ticket references, and code comments name `0.117.0` |
